compiled_state.title: ticket-unblock.py: v2 closed-status artifacts in _CLOSED_STATUSES -> unblock logic may mismatch v3
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
ticket-unblock.py line 64 defines: `_CLOSED_STATUSES = {"closed", "done", "resolved", "cancelled", "wont_fix"}`. The v3 ticket system only uses 'closed' as the terminal status. The extra values ('done', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'wont_fix') are v2 artifacts. While they cause no false-negatives (broader set is safe for unblocking), they indicate the file has not been audited for v3 compatibility.

## Impact
Low — tickets are only unblocked when their blockers are in _CLOSED_STATUSES, so extra statuses just make the set permissive. But if v3 ever adds new terminal statuses, this file may be missed in updates because it already looks 'handled'.

## Affected File
plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-unblock.py lines 63-65

## Fix
Verify that v3 only uses 'closed' as terminal status, then narrow to: `_CLOSED_STATUSES = {"closed"}`

comments.body: Classification: mechanical — narrowing _CLOSED_STATUSES to {"closed"} only; v2 artifacts ('done', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'wont_fix') removed
comments.author: Test
